channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026: Up to $1.5 Million in Funding for High-Growth Small and Medium Enterprises Across Africa, Asia, and Latin America
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are widely recognized as engines of economic growth, innovation, and job creation. However, many promising businesses in emerging markets struggle to access the financing and technical support needed to expand operations, enter new markets, adopt new technologies, and build long-term resilience. To help address these challenges, the SME Growth Accelerator […]

The post SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026: Up to $1.5 Million in Funding for High-Growth Small and Medium Enterprises Across Africa, Asia, and Latin America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) are widely recognized as engines of economic growth, innovation, and job creation. However, many promising businesses in emerging markets struggle to access the financing and technical support needed to expand operations, enter new markets, adopt new technologies, and build long-term resilience.

To help address these challenges, the SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026 is now accepting applications. This funding opportunity provides between USD 50,000 and USD 1.5 million in grant funding, alongside mentorship, technical assistance, networking opportunities, and access to potential follow-on financing for high-potential SMEs operating across Africa, Asia, and Latin America.

The programme is specifically designed to support for-profit businesses with strong growth potential that are committed to creating quality jobs, strengthening local economies, and promoting sustainable and inclusive development.

Applications are currently open, and eligible SMEs are encouraged to submit their applications before the 15 November 2026 deadline.

About the SME Growth Accelerator Grant

The SME Growth Accelerator is a grant programme focused on supporting ambitious small and medium-sized enterprises operating in emerging markets.

The programme aims to help businesses overcome common barriers to growth by providing both financial support and strategic business assistance.

Rather than simply offering funding, the initiative combines capital with mentorship, technical support, and business networking to strengthen participating companies and improve their long-term competitiveness.

Successful applicants will gain access to resources that can help them:

  • Scale business operations
  • Expand into new domestic and international markets
  • Increase productivity
  • Strengthen organizational capacity
  • Improve sustainability practices
  • Create quality employment opportunities
  • Prepare for future investment opportunities

The programme recognizes that SMEs play a vital role in driving inclusive economic growth, innovation, and sustainable development across emerging economies.

Funding Available

Successful applicants may receive grant funding ranging from:

  • Minimum Funding: USD 50,000
  • Maximum Funding: USD 1.5 million

The amount awarded will depend on the proposed project, business needs, scalability, and expected development impact.

What the Grant Supports

The SME Growth Accelerator provides flexible funding across several business development areas.

Eligible projects may include the following:

1. Working Capital

Businesses can use funding to strengthen operational capacity and support day-to-day business activities that facilitate growth.

Examples include:

  • Business expansion
  • Operational scaling
  • Inventory financing
  • Growth initiatives
  • Pilot project implementation

2. Technical Assistance and Capacity Building

The programme supports initiatives that improve business performance through technical expertise and organizational development.

This may include:

  • Staff development
  • Business process improvement
  • Operational efficiency
  • Management systems
  • Organizational strengthening

3. Climate and ESG-Aligned Investments

The grant encourages investments that improve environmental sustainability and responsible business practices.

Examples include:

  • Climate-smart technologies
  • Sustainability initiatives
  • Environmental management systems
  • ESG implementation
  • Resource efficiency projects

4. Market Research and Feasibility Studies

Funding may also support businesses seeking to better understand new markets before expanding.

Examples include:

  • Market analysis
  • Customer research
  • Expansion feasibility studies
  • Product validation
  • Market entry planning

5. Equipment and Technology Procurement

Businesses can invest in equipment and technology that improve productivity and competitiveness.

Examples include:

  • Machinery
  • Digital technologies
  • Business software
  • Manufacturing equipment
  • Productivity tools

6. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning

Applicants may allocate part of the funding toward measuring project outcomes and strengthening organizational learning.

Activities may include:

  • Impact measurement
  • Data collection
  • Performance monitoring
  • Learning systems
  • Evaluation frameworks

Programme Benefits

The SME Growth Accelerator provides significantly more than financial support.

Successful applicants will benefit from several valuable opportunities designed to strengthen long-term business success.

These include:

Grant Funding

Businesses may receive between USD 50,000 and USD 1.5 million to implement approved growth projects.

Technical Assistance

Participants receive expert support that helps improve operations, strengthen internal capacity, and implement business growth strategies.

Mentorship

The programme connects participating SMEs with experienced mentors who provide strategic guidance throughout the growth journey.

Business Networks

Successful applicants gain access to valuable business networks that may create opportunities for:

  • Partnerships
  • Collaboration
  • Business development
  • Market expansion

Follow-On Financing Opportunities

High-performing businesses may also gain access to future financing opportunities that support continued expansion beyond the grant period.

Who Can Apply?

The SME Growth Accelerator is open to eligible small and medium-sized enterprises operating in emerging markets.

Applicants must meet all programme eligibility requirements.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must meet the following conditions.

Business Type

Applicants must be:

  • For-profit small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs)

Number of Employees

Eligible businesses must employ between:

  • 10 and 250 employees

Annual Revenue

Applicants must demonstrate verified annual revenue between:

  • USD 250,000 and USD 10 million

Geographic Eligibility

The programme is open to eligible SMEs operating within:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Latin America

Why SMEs Should Apply

Many businesses possess strong products and services but lack sufficient capital and strategic support to reach their full potential.

The SME Growth Accelerator addresses this challenge by combining financial resources with business development services that enable companies to grow sustainably.

Participating businesses can use the programme to:

  • Expand production
  • Enter new markets
  • Improve competitiveness
  • Build organizational capacity
  • Strengthen sustainability practices
  • Generate employment
  • Increase long-term resilience

For SMEs with ambitious growth plans, this programme offers an opportunity to accelerate business development while contributing to broader economic growth within their countries.

Application Process

The application process has been designed to be simple and efficient.

Applicants can complete the submission online.

According to the programme information:

  • The application takes approximately 15 minutes to complete.
  • There are no application fees.
  • Applications are reviewed according to established World Bank Group standards.
  • Applicants can expect a decision within 4 to 6 weeks after submission.

Interested businesses should ensure they prepare all required business information before beginning the application.

APPLY HERE

Important Dates

Applicants should note the following timeline:

  • Application Status: Open
  • Application Deadline: 15 November 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

Why This Grant Matters

Access to growth capital remains one of the greatest barriers facing SMEs across emerging markets.

Without adequate financing, many businesses struggle to:

  • Expand production
  • Hire additional staff
  • Purchase equipment
  • Improve operational systems
  • Enter new markets
  • Adopt sustainable technologies

The SME Growth Accelerator directly addresses these challenges by combining grant funding with mentorship, technical assistance, and networking opportunities.

Its emphasis on sustainable growth, climate resilience, ESG alignment, and inclusive economic development makes it particularly valuable for businesses seeking long-term impact.

By supporting SMEs that demonstrate strong growth potential, the programme contributes to job creation, economic resilience, innovation, and sustainable private-sector development across Africa, Asia, and Latin America.

Tips for Preparing a Strong Application

To maximize the likelihood of success, applicants should:

  • Clearly explain their business model and growth strategy.
  • Demonstrate measurable business performance and verified annual revenue.
  • Present a realistic budget aligned with eligible funding activities.
  • Highlight expected social, economic, or environmental impact.
  • Show how grant funding will contribute to sustainable business growth.
  • Explain plans for job creation, innovation, or market expansion.
  • Submit the application well before the closing date.

Visit HERE to learn more about the SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026.

Conclusion

The SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for high-potential small and medium-sized enterprises across Africa, Asia, and Latin America to secure substantial funding and strategic business support. Offering grants ranging from USD 50,000 to USD 1.5 million, the programme goes beyond financial assistance by providing mentorship, technical assistance, access to professional networks, and opportunities for follow-on financing.

Whether your business is looking to expand into new markets, invest in technology, strengthen sustainability practices, or create quality jobs, this programme offers valuable resources to accelerate long-term growth. With a streamlined online application process, no application fees, and decisions typically made within four to six weeks, eligible SMEs should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the 15 November 2026 deadline.

Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY.

The post SME Growth Accelerator Grant 2026: Up to $1.5 Million in Funding for High-Growth Small and Medium Enterprises Across Africa, Asia, and Latin America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Doctoral Student in IMER with a Focus on Survey Studies of Diversity and Inclusion at Malmö University, Sweden | Fully Funded PhD Opportunity 2027
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are passionate about migration studies, diversity, inclusion, social sciences, and quantitative research, Malmö University is offering an outstanding opportunity to pursue a fully funded doctoral studentship in International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER). This prestigious PhD position is hosted by the Department of Global Political Studies within the Faculty of Culture and Society […]

The post Doctoral Student in IMER with a Focus on Survey Studies of Diversity and Inclusion at Malmö University, Sweden | Fully Funded PhD Opportunity 2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about migration studies, diversity, inclusion, social sciences, and quantitative research, Malmö University is offering an outstanding opportunity to pursue a fully funded doctoral studentship in International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER). This prestigious PhD position is hosted by the Department of Global Political Studies within the Faculty of Culture and Society and provides aspiring researchers with the chance to contribute to one of Sweden’s leading migration research environments.

The doctoral studentship is closely linked to the Migration Society 2.0 research programme and the internationally recognized Malmö Institute for Migration Studies (MIM). Successful applicants will undertake an independent research project focused on survey studies of diversity and inclusion while participating in a vibrant interdisciplinary research community.

The programme is designed for candidates who wish to build careers in academia, research, public policy, migration studies, international development, or social sciences.

Application Deadline: 31 August 2026

About Malmö University

Malmö University is one of Sweden’s most innovative and internationally oriented higher education institutions. Located in the vibrant city of Malmö, the university brings together scholars, researchers, educators, and professionals from around the world to produce high-quality education and research that contributes to societal development.

The university promotes collaboration across disciplines and values diversity, equality, innovation, and inclusion. Employees and doctoral students benefit from an international academic environment that encourages interdisciplinary research and strong engagement with society.

About the Doctoral Position

The Department of Global Political Studies (GPS) is seeking one doctoral student in International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER).

The selected candidate will become part of a multidisciplinary research environment dedicated to understanding migration, ethnicity, integration, governance, politics, identity, and global social change.

The doctoral project will focus on survey-based research exploring diversity and inclusion within Sweden through the Migration Society 2.0 programme.

Research Focus: Diversity and Inclusion Survey Studies

The doctoral research is part of the ambitious Migration Society 2.0 research programme, which seeks to deepen understanding of the increasingly diverse nature of Swedish society.

The project investigates the concept of superdiversity, recognizing that modern migration has created societies characterized not only by increasing numbers of migrants but also by growing diversity in:

  • Ethnicity
  • National origin
  • Migration pathways
  • Legal status
  • Gender
  • Age
  • Language
  • Religion
  • Social class
  • Sexuality
  • Transnational connections

To better understand these societal transformations, Malmö University is developing a large-scale Diversity and Inclusion Survey, which will be repeated every two years to create an expanding database for future research.

The survey will collect self-identified information relating to:

  • Ethnicity
  • Gender identity
  • Religion
  • Language
  • Sexual orientation
  • Social status
  • Sense of belonging
  • Attitudes toward other groups
  • Perceptions of social institutions
  • Experiences of inclusion and exclusion

The successful doctoral student will design and conduct an original research project using data generated from this survey.

Applicants are expected to submit a strong research proposal demonstrating theoretical originality, methodological rigor, and a clear vision for investigating diversity and inclusion through survey-based research.

About the IMER Doctoral Programme

The Doctoral Programme in International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER) is an interdisciplinary programme examining migration and its consequences from structural, institutional, collective, and individual perspectives.

Researchers within IMER explore topics including:

  • International migration
  • Refugee movements
  • Integration
  • Ethnic relations
  • Identity
  • Social inclusion
  • Governance
  • Citizenship
  • Social inequality
  • Cultural diversity
  • Global mobility

The programme integrates knowledge from numerous disciplines, including:

  • Sociology
  • Political Science
  • Anthropology
  • Cultural Geography
  • Economics
  • Economic History
  • History
  • Social Work
  • Gender Studies
  • Religious Studies
  • Ethnology
  • Cultural Studies

Programme Structure

The doctoral programme comprises 240 higher education credits, equivalent to four years of full-time study.

The programme includes:

  • 60 credits of doctoral coursework
  • 180-credit independent doctoral dissertation
  • Active participation in seminars
  • Research meetings
  • Departmental activities
  • Collaboration with fellow researchers
  • Public defense of the doctoral dissertation

Doctoral students are expected to actively contribute to the academic environment at Malmö University and the Malmö Institute for Migration Studies.

Employment Responsibilities

The primary responsibility of the doctoral student is to pursue doctoral studies and conduct independent research.

However, doctoral students may also contribute to:

  • Teaching
  • Educational activities
  • Research assistance
  • Academic administration

These additional responsibilities cannot exceed 20% of a full-time position, in accordance with Sweden’s Higher Education Ordinance.

Research Environment

The successful applicant will become part of the internationally renowned Malmö Institute for Migration Studies (MIM).

Established in 2007, MIM serves as a leading international research centre focusing on migration studies. It brings together senior and junior researchers while maintaining collaborations with scholars from around the world.

The institute regularly hosts internationally recognized professors and promotes interdisciplinary research addressing migration, diversity, and inclusion.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must satisfy both the general and specific entry requirements for doctoral studies.

General Entry Requirements

Applicants must meet one of the following criteria:

  • Hold a second-cycle (Master’s) qualification.
  • Have completed at least 240 higher education credits, including 60 credits at second-cycle level.
  • Possess substantially equivalent qualifications obtained in Sweden or abroad.

Specific Entry Requirements

Applicants should possess academic qualifications equivalent to a second-cycle degree in:

  • International Migration and Ethnic Relations (IMER), or
  • Another discipline closely related to IMER.

Relevant academic backgrounds include:

  • Sociology
  • Political Science
  • Anthropology
  • Cultural Geography
  • Economics
  • Economic History
  • History
  • Social Work
  • Ethnology
  • Religious Studies
  • Gender Studies
  • Cultural Studies

Selection Criteria

Admission is based on the applicant’s ability to successfully complete doctoral education.

Selection will consider:

  1. Independent analytical ability.
  2. Originality of previous academic work.
  3. Quality of the proposed research plan.
  4. Clarity of research questions.
  5. Theoretical understanding.
  6. Methodological competence.
  7. Scholarly maturity.
  8. Ability to conduct independent research.

Additional advantages include:

  • Knowledge of quantitative research methods.
  • Statistical analysis skills.
  • Survey methodology experience.
  • Experience using statistical software.
  • Database management experience.
  • Data visualization expertise.
  • Experience working with register or survey datasets.

Application Documents

Applicants must submit a complete application that includes:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Certified academic transcripts and diplomas
  • Relevant certificates
  • Research plan (maximum 3,000 words excluding references)
  • Master’s thesis or equivalent independent research work
  • Personal letter (maximum 500 words) highlighting research experience, academic interests, and experience working with survey data

Applications may be submitted in:

  • English
  • Swedish
  • Any Nordic language

Applicants are responsible for ensuring all required documents are translated where necessary.

Employment Conditions

The doctoral studentship offers:

  • Four years of full-time doctoral study
  • Fixed-term doctoral employment
  • Salary according to Malmö University’s doctoral salary agreement
  • Possibility of teaching and academic duties (up to 20%)
  • Access to Sweden’s internationally recognized research environment
  • Academic supervision
  • Professional development opportunities
  • International research collaboration

The initial appointment is normally for one year and may be extended in accordance with Swedish regulations.

Equality and Inclusion

Malmö University actively promotes equal opportunities and an inclusive academic environment.

The university encourages:

  • Gender equality
  • Equal opportunities for all applicants
  • Support for doctoral students with disabilities
  • Inclusive participation in research and education

Applicants from an underrepresented gender may receive priority when candidates are equally qualified.

International employees who do not speak Swedish are encouraged to learn the language over time to facilitate participation in university life.

Residence Permit Information

International students requiring a residence permit to study in Sweden must obtain a valid permit before commencing the programme.

Failure to secure the required residence permit before the start date may result in the admission being withdrawn.

Programme Start Date

1 February 2027

Why You Should Apply

This doctoral studentship provides an exceptional opportunity to conduct cutting-edge research on migration, diversity, and inclusion while studying at one of Europe’s leading migration research institutions.

Benefits include:

  • Fully funded doctoral employment
  • Four years of funded PhD study
  • Monthly doctoral salary
  • Participation in an internationally renowned research centre
  • Access to large-scale survey datasets
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Academic supervision from leading migration scholars
  • Strong international research networks
  • Professional teaching and research experience
  • Career preparation for academia, public policy, NGOs, and international organizations

Apply Here

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted no later than 31 August 2026.

Candidates should ensure that all required documents are uploaded through Malmö University’s recruitment system before the deadline.

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global PhD scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Doctoral Student in IMER with a Focus on Survey Studies of Diversity and Inclusion at Malmö University, Sweden | Fully Funded PhD Opportunity 2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNCAC Coalition Hiring Regional Coordinators for SWANA & Latin America 2026 | Remote International Consultancy Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Global Civil Society Coalition for the UN Convention Against Corruption (UNCAC Coalition) has officially opened applications for two major international consultancy opportunities for professionals working in anti-corruption, governance, civil society engagement, advocacy, and human rights. The organization is currently recruiting: Regional Coordinator for Southwest Asia and North Africa (SWANA/MENA) Regional Coordinator for Latin America […]

The post UNCAC Coalition Hiring Regional Coordinators for SWANA & Latin America 2026 | Remote International Consultancy Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Civil Society Coalition for the UN Convention Against Corruption (UNCAC Coalition) has officially opened applications for two major international consultancy opportunities for professionals working in anti-corruption, governance, civil society engagement, advocacy, and human rights.

The organization is currently recruiting:

  1. Regional Coordinator for Southwest Asia and North Africa (SWANA/MENA)
  2. Regional Coordinator for Latin America & the Caribbean (LAC)

These are remote, part-time consultancy roles designed for experienced professionals who are passionate about strengthening transparency, accountability, anti-corruption systems, and civil society participation across their respective regions.

The opportunities provide selected consultants with the chance to collaborate with a globally recognized civil society network that works directly on issues related to the United Nations Convention against Corruption (UNCAC), the world’s only legally binding universal anti-corruption instrument.

About the UNCAC Coalition

The UNCAC Coalition is a global network consisting of more than 400 civil society organizations committed to promoting transparency, accountability, anti-corruption reforms, access to information, financial transparency, and human rights protections worldwide.

The Coalition works to strengthen the implementation and monitoring of the United Nations Convention against Corruption by connecting civil society actors, advocacy groups, researchers, policy experts, and governance organizations from around the world.

Its work includes:

  • Advocacy and policy engagement
  • Civil society coordination
  • Monitoring UNCAC implementation
  • Organizing trainings and workshops
  • Producing research and reports
  • Promoting transparency and public participation
  • Supporting anti-corruption reforms globally

The Coalition operates internationally and collaborates with stakeholders across governments, civil society organizations, regional institutions, and international organizations.

Available Positions

1. Regional Coordinator – Southwest Asia & North Africa (SWANA/MENA)

The Coalition is seeking a consultant to coordinate outreach, communications, advocacy, and civil society engagement activities across the SWANA/MENA region.

The selected consultant will help strengthen relationships with civil society organizations working on anti-corruption and governance issues while also supporting regional advocacy initiatives connected to UNCAC implementation.

2. Regional Coordinator – Latin America & the Caribbean (LAC)

The Coalition is also recruiting a consultant for the Latin America and Caribbean region to support regional anti-corruption engagement, civil society coordination, communications, networking, and research activities.

This role focuses on expanding and strengthening the Coalition’s presence and collaboration across Latin America and the Caribbean.

Position Type and Work Structure

Both opportunities are:

  • Remote positions
  • Part-time consultancy roles
  • International opportunities
  • Long-term consultancy engagements
  • Flexible working arrangements

Estimated Weekly Workload

Selected consultants are expected to work approximately:

  • 6 to 10 hours per week

Candidates who are flexible with their schedules and available for regional coordination activities are strongly preferred.

Compensation and Benefits

The consultancy roles offer:

  • Estimated compensation of €20 per hour
  • Potential for higher compensation depending on expertise and experience
  • Flexible remote work arrangements
  • International networking opportunities
  • Opportunity to work with global anti-corruption experts and organizations
  • Participation in regional and international advocacy activities
  • Possible travel opportunities for UNCAC-related events

International Conference Participation

Selected consultants may also:

  • Participate in the UNCAC Conference of the States Parties
  • Receive approximately 10 days of covered travel expenses for major UNCAC events
  • Represent the Coalition at regional anti-corruption meetings, depending on funding availability

Main Responsibilities

The Regional Coordinators will support a wide range of activities focused on strengthening anti-corruption civil society engagement in their regions.

Key Responsibilities Include:

Civil Society Network Development

  • Expand and strengthen regional networks of civil society organizations
  • Identify relevant anti-corruption organizations in the region
  • Encourage organizations to join the Coalition
  • Build stronger collaboration among regional members

Regional Coordination

  • Serve as a point of contact for regional civil society organizations
  • Liaise between regional organizations and the Vienna Hub team
  • Moderate regional email discussion groups
  • Facilitate collaboration among members

Events and Meetings

  • Organize and moderate 4–5 online regional meetings annually
  • Identify speakers and discussion themes
  • Coordinate workshops and trainings
  • Support virtual advocacy events

Advocacy and Research

  • Monitor anti-corruption developments in the region
  • Conduct outreach to governments and civil society actors
  • Support transparency and accountability initiatives
  • Contribute to research and advocacy activities

Content and Communications

  • Draft briefing notes and reports
  • Produce blog posts and written submissions
  • Moderate regional discussions
  • Share updates and advocacy materials

Membership and UNCAC Engagement

  • Support civil society participation in UNCAC review processes
  • Encourage production of civil society parallel reports
  • Help administer membership applications
  • Promote transparency in UNCAC implementation reviews

Required Qualifications

SWANA/MENA Regional Coordinator

Applicants must:

  • Speak and write English and Arabic fluently
  • Have strong knowledge of anti-corruption and governance issues in the region
  • Possess at least 2 years of experience working with civil society organizations
  • Be familiar with political and governance developments in the SWANA region
  • Demonstrate strong writing, communication, and moderation skills
  • Be self-motivated, organized, diplomatic, and reliable
  • Preferably be based within the SWANA region

Latin America & Caribbean Regional Coordinator

Applicants must:

  • Speak and write English and Spanish fluently
  • Have experience with civil society organizations in Latin America and the Caribbean
  • Demonstrate strong understanding of anti-corruption and governance issues
  • Be capable of coordinating regional advocacy activities
  • Possess excellent communication and networking skills
  • Show strong organizational and moderation abilities
  • Preferably be based within the LAC region

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following:

  1. CV in English
    • Maximum two pages
    • PDF format
    • No pictures
  2. One-page Motivation Letter in English
    • PDF format
    • Mention availability
    • Mention potential conflicts of interest
  3. Writing Sample
    • Maximum two pages
    • In English
    • Ideally existing professional work
  4. AI Disclosure
    • Applicants must disclose any use of AI tools in preparing application materials

Application Process

Applicants must:

  • Complete the official application form
  • Email all required documents to:
    jobs@uncaccoalition.org

Email Subject Lines

  • “Regional Coordinator – SWANA Region”
  • “Regional Coordinator – Latin America & the Caribbean Region”

Application Deadlines

SWANA / MENA Position

Deadline:
Monday, 10 August 2026

Latin America & Caribbean Position

Deadline:
Monday, 27 July 2026 (Midnight CEST)

Starting Date

Successful candidates are expected to begin:

  • As soon as possible

Why This Opportunity Matters

These positions offer a valuable chance for professionals in governance, anti-corruption, advocacy, and civil society engagement to contribute to global transparency and accountability efforts.

The selected consultants will work directly with one of the world’s leading anti-corruption civil society coalitions while helping shape regional advocacy, strengthen networks, and support international anti-corruption reforms.

Professionals interested in governance, public policy, human rights, international development, and transparency initiatives will find these roles highly impactful and career-enhancing.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply for the SWANA Regional Coordinator Application HERE.

Interested applicants can apply for the Latin American and the Caribbean Regional Coordinator HERE.

Click HERE for more information on the UNCAC Coalition for SWANA & Latin America 2026 job opportunities.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post UNCAC Coalition Hiring Regional Coordinators for SWANA & Latin America 2026 | Remote International Consultancy Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WHO & CJID Launch African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026 for Journalists in Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ethiopia & South Africa
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The World Health Organization (WHO) and the Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID) have officially launched a groundbreaking initiative aimed at transforming how African media organizations report on road safety and public health issues across the continent. Applications are now open for the African Road Safety Reporting Forum, a high-level three-day gathering scheduled to […]

The post WHO & CJID Launch African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026 for Journalists in Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ethiopia & South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Health Organization (WHO) and the Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID) have officially launched a groundbreaking initiative aimed at transforming how African media organizations report on road safety and public health issues across the continent.

Applications are now open for the African Road Safety Reporting Forum, a high-level three-day gathering scheduled to take place in Abuja, Nigeria, in September 2026. The forum will bring together 20 editors, journalists, and media influencers from across Africa to develop the continent’s first-ever editorial and reporting guidelines focused specifically on road safety reporting.

This important initiative seeks to address one of Africa’s most urgent yet underreported public health crises — road traffic crashes and fatalities.

Deadline for Applications

The application deadline is 10 July 2026.

About the African Road Safety Reporting Forum

The African Road Safety Reporting Forum is a collaborative initiative between the World Health Organization (WHO) and the Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID). The programme is designed to strengthen journalism practices around road safety reporting and improve public understanding of road crashes as preventable public health and governance challenges.

The forum will be hosted in Abuja, Nigeria, and will gather selected participants from Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ethiopia, South Africa, and potentially other African countries.

Over the course of three days, participants will work directly with:

  • WHO technical experts
  • Leading African editors
  • Media development specialists
  • Public health communication professionals

Together, they will create industry-standard reporting guidelines that can shape how African newsrooms cover road safety issues for years to come.

Why This Initiative Matters

Road traffic crashes are currently the leading cause of death among children and young people aged 5 to 29 globally. According to WHO data, Africa carries one of the heaviest burdens of road fatalities in the world.

Key statistics highlighted by the organizers include:

  • Africa is the only WHO region where road deaths continue to rise
  • Road deaths in Africa increased by 17% between 2010 and 2021
  • The continent accounts for nearly one in five road deaths globally
  • Africa has the highest road traffic fatality rate worldwide
  • The region represents only 15% of the global population, yet carries a disproportionate share of road crash deaths

Despite these alarming realities, road safety reporting often receives limited media attention or is framed incorrectly.

Organizers argue that many crash reports:

  • Treat road crashes as unavoidable accidents
  • Focus only on individual blame
  • Ignore systemic causes such as poor infrastructure
  • Overlook policy failures and governance gaps
  • Fail to investigate long-term public health impacts

The forum aims to change this narrative by promoting evidence-based, accountability-driven, and solutions-focused journalism.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected participants will receive several professional and practical benefits throughout the programme.

Benefits of the Forum

Participants will receive:

  1. Full travel sponsorship
  2. Accommodation support
  3. Daily stipends
  4. Professional journalism training
  5. Access to WHO technical experts
  6. Networking opportunities with top African editors
  7. Participation in developing Africa’s first road safety reporting standards
  8. Opportunity to produce impactful road safety stories
  9. Continued collaboration through an online working group after the event

Focus Areas of the Training

The initiative will equip journalists and editors with tools and frameworks to improve reporting quality in areas such as:

  • Public health journalism
  • Solutions-focused reporting
  • Accountability journalism
  • Investigative reporting on road safety
  • Data-driven storytelling
  • Ethical reporting standards
  • Governance and infrastructure reporting
  • Policy-focused journalism
  • Audience-centered reporting

Participants will also work on practical editorial projects and real reporting exercises during the forum.

Statements from Organizers and Partners

Akintunde Babatunde — Executive Director, CJID

Akintunde Babatunde described road safety as one of Africa’s most critical but underreported public health challenges.

According to him, the way societies understand road crashes influences:

  • Public opinion
  • Government accountability
  • Policy reform
  • Infrastructure investment
  • Safety interventions

He explained that the partnership between CJID and WHO aims to strengthen evidence-based reporting and help create safer roads across Africa.

Ifeanyi Chukwudi — African Road Safety Reporting Initiative Lead

Ifeanyi Chukwudi emphasized the important role journalists play in shaping how the public and policymakers understand road safety issues.

He noted that the initiative seeks to provide African journalists with:

  • Better evidence
  • Reporting tools
  • Shared editorial standards
  • Stronger public health frameworks

This, he said, will help reporters treat road crashes as systemic and solvable issues rather than isolated incidents.

Matthew Taylor — Consulting Project Manager, WHO

Matthew Taylor highlighted that Africa remains the only global region where road fatalities are still increasing.

He stressed that improving public understanding through responsible journalism is essential for reducing road deaths and promoting effective solutions.

WHO, he said, is proud to support African newsrooms in building accurate and accountability-driven coverage focused on life-saving interventions.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is ideal for:

  • Editors
  • Journalists
  • Newsroom leaders
  • Media influencers
  • Investigative reporters
  • Public health journalists
  • Development communication professionals

Applicants with demonstrated interest in:

  • Public health
  • Governance
  • Accountability journalism
  • Development reporting
  • Road safety reporting

are strongly encouraged to apply.

Priority Countries

Priority consideration will be given to applicants from:

  • Kenya
  • Nigeria
  • Uganda
  • Ethiopia
  • South Africa

However, strong applications from other African countries may also be considered.

About CJID

The Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID) is one of Africa’s leading media innovation and development organizations focused on strengthening journalism, democracy, accountability, and information integrity across the continent.

CJID works extensively in:

  • Investigative journalism
  • Media innovation
  • Fact-checking and verification
  • Open data
  • Elections
  • Digital governance
  • Freedom of expression
  • Journalist safety
  • Public health reporting

Its Health Reporting Project specifically supports journalists and newsrooms in producing deeper, more accurate, and more impactful reporting on major public health and development issues.

Final Thoughts

The African Road Safety Reporting Forum represents a major step toward improving public interest journalism and public health reporting across Africa.

By bringing together editors, journalists, media innovators, and WHO experts, the initiative seeks to build stronger newsroom standards, improve accountability reporting, and reshape how African societies understand road safety.

For journalists passionate about impactful storytelling, public health communication, and policy-focused reporting, this forum offers a unique opportunity to contribute to a historic continental initiative that could help save lives and transform media narratives across Africa.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the Road Safety Reporting Forum.

Explore more global opportunities on OFY here.

The post WHO & CJID Launch African Road Safety Reporting Forum 2026 for Journalists in Kenya, Nigeria, Uganda, Ethiopia & South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI and the Youth Labour Market Research Grant 2026: Apply for Up to £120,000 Funding from the Youth Futures Foundation
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Youth Futures Foundation is inviting research organisations, universities, think tanks, consultancies, and other eligible research partners to apply for its AI and the Youth Labour Market Call for Proposals. This funding opportunity offers up to £120,000 to support a 10-month research project that will investigate how artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming employment opportunities, skills […]

The post AI and the Youth Labour Market Research Grant 2026: Apply for Up to £120,000 Funding from the Youth Futures Foundation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Youth Futures Foundation is inviting research organisations, universities, think tanks, consultancies, and other eligible research partners to apply for its AI and the Youth Labour Market Call for Proposals. This funding opportunity offers up to £120,000 to support a 10-month research project that will investigate how artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming employment opportunities, skills demand, and labour market outcomes for young people aged 16–24 in England.

As AI continues to reshape industries across the world, understanding its impact on young people’s employment prospects has become increasingly important. Through this research call, the Youth Futures Foundation seeks evidence-based insights that can help policymakers, employers, educators, and funders develop effective strategies to ensure young people—especially those facing barriers to employment—can thrive in an AI-driven labour market.

The selected research partner will examine the opportunities and risks associated with AI, particularly its influence on entry-level jobs, career progression, skills requirements, and inequalities affecting young people who are not in education, employment, or training (NEET).

Application Deadline: 20 July 2026

About the Youth Futures Foundation

The Youth Futures Foundation is an independent, evidence-focused organisation committed to improving employment outcomes for young people across England. The foundation works with researchers, employers, policymakers, charities, and community organisations to identify effective solutions that reduce youth unemployment and improve access to meaningful work.

Through research, innovation, and strategic partnerships, Youth Futures Foundation supports initiatives that generate high-quality evidence capable of shaping national policies and improving opportunities for disadvantaged young people.

This latest research funding opportunity reflects the organisation’s commitment to understanding how emerging technologies, particularly artificial intelligence, will influence the future of work for the next generation.

About the AI and the Youth Labour Market Research Project

Artificial intelligence is rapidly changing how businesses operate, the types of jobs available, and the skills employers require. While AI presents significant opportunities for innovation and productivity, it also raises concerns about job displacement, workforce inequality, and changing career pathways.

The Youth Futures Foundation is commissioning new research to better understand these changes and their implications for young people entering the labour market.

The successful research project will generate robust evidence that informs future policies and interventions aimed at supporting young people through this period of technological transformation.

Research Objectives

The funded research will explore several important questions surrounding AI and employment.

Key areas of investigation include:

1. How AI is Reshaping Entry-Level Employment

Researchers are expected to examine:

  • The impact of AI on entry-level occupations.
  • Emerging employment opportunities created by AI.
  • Jobs and sectors most vulnerable to automation.
  • Changes in recruitment practices.
  • Future career pathways for young workers.

2. Skills Demand in an AI-Driven Economy

The research should investigate:

  • Skills becoming increasingly valuable because of AI.
  • Skills that may become less relevant.
  • Digital and technical competencies required by employers.
  • Adaptability and lifelong learning needs.
  • Future workforce readiness.

3. Career Progression and Labour Market Mobility

Applicants should consider:

  • How AI influences career advancement.
  • New opportunities for professional development.
  • Barriers affecting career progression.
  • Long-term employment prospects for young workers.

4. Distributional Impacts of Artificial Intelligence

The project should explore how AI affects different groups of young people, including whether certain communities experience greater benefits or disadvantages.

Researchers are encouraged to examine:

  • Regional differences.
  • Socioeconomic inequalities.
  • Demographic disparities.
  • Access to digital opportunities.
  • Inclusion and equity.

5. Young People Who Are NEET

A major focus of the project is understanding how AI influences young people who are:

  • Not in Education
  • Not in Employment
  • Not in Training (NEET)

Researchers should investigate how AI may either increase employment barriers or create new pathways into meaningful work, particularly for young people who are furthest from the labour market.

Why This Research Matters

Artificial intelligence is expected to transform nearly every sector of the economy. However, there remains limited evidence about how these changes specifically affect young people transitioning into employment.

The findings from this research will help:

  • Government policymakers.
  • Employers.
  • Educational institutions.
  • Workforce development organisations.
  • Charities.
  • Research institutions.
  • Funding organisations.

The evidence generated will support informed decision-making and help reduce the risk that AI-driven labour market changes widen existing inequalities.

Funding Details

Successful applicants can receive:

  • Funding of up to £120,000
  • Project duration of 10 months
  • Research period running from August 2026 to June 2027

The funding is intended to support high-quality research that delivers practical recommendations for improving youth employment outcomes in the context of artificial intelligence.

Who the Youth Futures Foundation Wants to Partner With

The Foundation is seeking experienced research partners capable of delivering rigorous, policy-relevant research.

Suitable applicants may include:

  • Universities
  • Research institutes
  • Independent research organisations
  • Think tanks
  • Policy research consultancies
  • Evaluation organisations
  • Collaborative research partnerships

Applicants should demonstrate expertise in labour market research, artificial intelligence, youth employment, quantitative and qualitative research methods, policy evaluation, or related disciplines.

Expected Research Impact

The successful project should produce findings that:

  • Strengthen understanding of AI’s labour market effects.
  • Inform evidence-based policymaking.
  • Support employers adapting recruitment strategies.
  • Improve workforce development programmes.
  • Reduce employment inequalities.
  • Enhance opportunities for disadvantaged young people.
  • Guide future investment in youth employment initiatives.

Benefits of This Opportunity

Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • Research funding of up to £120,000.
  • Opportunity to influence national policy.
  • Collaboration with the Youth Futures Foundation.
  • Contribution to one of the UK’s most important discussions on AI and employment.
  • Increased visibility within research and policy communities.
  • Opportunity to shape future youth employment strategies.

How to Apply

Applicants should carefully review the official application pack before preparing their proposal.

A strong proposal should clearly demonstrate:

  1. Relevant research expertise.
  2. Understanding of AI and labour market dynamics.
  3. Experience in youth employment research.
  4. Robust research methodology.
  5. Capacity to deliver within the 10-month timeframe.
  6. Ability to produce policy-relevant recommendations.

Applications should be submitted before the published deadline.

Project Timeline

  • Application Deadline: 20 July 2026
  • Project Duration: 10 months
  • Project Period: August 2026 – June 2027
  • Location: United Kingdom (England-focused research)

Why You Should Apply

This is an exceptional opportunity for research organisations seeking to influence the future of work in the age of artificial intelligence.

With AI transforming industries at an unprecedented pace, the findings generated through this project will help shape employment policy, improve opportunities for young people, and contribute to reducing labour market inequalities across England.

Organisations with expertise in AI, economics, education, labour markets, public policy, and youth employment are strongly encouraged to apply.

Learn more and Apply Here

Application Deadline

Applications close on 20 July 2026.

Interested applicants should review the application pack thoroughly and submit their proposal before the deadline.

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post AI and the Youth Labour Market Research Grant 2026: Apply for Up to £120,000 Funding from the Youth Futures Foundation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Paid Traineeships at the European Economic and Social Committee (Open to all graduates and students in Europe)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Overview The European Economic and Social Committee (EESC) offers traineeships to university graduates and students twice a year. These opportunities are designed to provide professional experience and an understanding of the EESC’s roles and activities. Types of Traineeships Long-term Traineeships Duration: Five months Eligibility: University graduates Location: EESC offices in Brussels Compensation: €1,418.20 per month […]

The post Paid Traineeships at the European Economic and Social Committee (Open to all graduates and students in Europe) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Overview

The European Economic and Social Committee (EESC) offers traineeships to university graduates and students twice a year. These opportunities are designed to provide professional experience and an understanding of the EESC’s roles and activities.

Types of Traineeships

  1. Long-term Traineeships
    • Duration: Five months
    • Eligibility: University graduates
      • Location: EESC offices in Brussels
      • Compensation: €1,418.20 per month
      • Application Periods:
        • Autumn Session: Apply by 31 March
        • Spring Session: Apply by 30 September
  2. Short-term Traineeships
    • Duration: One to three months
    • Eligibility: University students requiring a traineeship for their studies
    • Compensation: 50% of the monthly grant for long-term trainees
    • Application: No fixed deadline; applications valid for six months

Benefits

  • Gain professional experience in a multicultural environment
  • Enhance research, writing, and event management skills
  • Network with experts in various fields

What is offered:

  • a monthly grant corresponding to 50% of the monthly amount granted to long-term trainees;
  • a mobility allowance;
  • a travel allowance (if applicable).

Application Process

Long-term Traineeships

  • Application Form: Complete the online application form. Please submit an online application form.
  • Required Documents: University diploma or equivalent, identity card or passport
  • Selection Process: Pre-selected candidates will be asked to submit additional documents, including a police record extract and medical certificate

Short-term Traineeships

  • Application Form: Complete the online application form. Please submit an online application form.
  • Required Documents: University enrollment certificate, proof of compulsory traineeship, health insurance, police record extract, and medical certificate

Important Considerations

  • Communication: Only contact the EESC traineeship office via email if necessary
  • Document Submission: Only submit documents when requested
  • Application Validity: Short-term applications remain valid for six months
  • Health and Accident Insurance: Mandatory for all trainees

Eligibility:

1. I haven’t got my diploma yet – can I apply?

To apply for a long-term traineeship, you must hold a recognised university-level degree of at least three years of studies, attested by a diploma by the closing date for applications.

2. I am 33 years old – can I apply?

Yes, there is no age limit.

To apply, please submit an online application form.

Your application will remain valid for six months. If we are in a position to offer you a traineeship during that period, we will get in touch with you and ask you to submit the following supporting documents:

  • a certificate confirming attendance at a university or a higher education establishment,
  • proof that such a traineeship is compulsory for the completion of your studies,
  • proof of valid health insurance,
  • an extract from police records, and
  • a medical certificate proving that you are medically fit to carry out the traineeship.

Please note: Short-term traineeships may only start on the 1st or 16th of a given month, except July and August. There will be at least two months between the submission of an application and the start of the traineeship.

Additional Information

Twice a year the European Economic and Social Committee offers traineeships for a period of five months to university graduates (long-term traineeship).

In addition, the EESC can offer short, paid traineeships of one, two or three months to university students for whom such a traineeship is compulsory for the completion of their studies.(short-term traineeship).

For more information:

  • Long-term traineeship
  • Short-term traineeship
  • Rules and application forms
  • Data protection notice
  • Frequently asked questions

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview. Please ensure your application is complete for consideration.

Source: https://www.eesc.europa.eu/en/work-with-us/traineeships/long-term-traineeships

The post Paid Traineeships at the European Economic and Social Committee (Open to all graduates and students in Europe) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Associate Project Officer (Learning Assessments) at UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS), Montreal, Canada: Apply for an International Education Data and Research Career
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS) is inviting applications for the position of Associate Project Officer (Learning Assessments). This is an exciting international career opportunity for professionals with expertise in statistics, education data, research, project management, and learning assessments who are passionate about improving education systems worldwide. Based in Montreal, Canada, this Project Appointment (P-2 […]

The post Associate Project Officer (Learning Assessments) at UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS), Montreal, Canada: Apply for an International Education Data and Research Career appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS) is inviting applications for the position of Associate Project Officer (Learning Assessments). This is an exciting international career opportunity for professionals with expertise in statistics, education data, research, project management, and learning assessments who are passionate about improving education systems worldwide.

Based in Montreal, Canada, this Project Appointment (P-2 Grade) offers qualified professionals the opportunity to contribute to the global monitoring of Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG 4) by supporting the collection, management, validation, and reporting of international learning assessment data.

The position is open to both internal and external candidates and offers a one-year contract, with the possibility of extension depending on funding availability and satisfactory performance.

Application Deadline: 8 July 2026 (Midnight Paris Time)

About UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS)

The UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS) serves as the official source of internationally comparable statistics on:

  • Education
  • Science
  • Culture
  • Communication

Within the education sector, UIS plays a leading role in measuring and tracking global progress toward Sustainable Development Goal 4 (SDG 4), which focuses on ensuring inclusive and equitable quality education and promoting lifelong learning opportunities for all.

As countries continue producing larger volumes of learning assessment data, UNESCO is modernizing its systems, methodologies, and data management processes to improve:

  • Data quality
  • Accessibility
  • Transparency
  • International comparability
  • Policy relevance

The Associate Project Officer will become part of this transformation by helping manage international datasets, supporting research, coordinating projects, and ensuring high-quality reporting of learning outcomes across countries.

Position Overview

Position Title: Associate Project Officer (Learning Assessments)

Position Number: 1CAUIS0107PA

Grade: P-2

Organization: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS)

Job Family: Education

Duty Station: Montreal, Canada

Contract Type: Project Appointment

Contract Duration: 1 year (renewable based on funding and performance)

Recruitment: Internal and External Candidates

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will contribute across several strategic areas.

1. Learning Assessment Programme Support

The Associate Project Officer will assist in implementing UNESCO’s learning assessment programme by:

  • Supporting production of the global learning indicator (SDG 4.1.1)
  • Applying data eligibility criteria
  • Supporting technical workflows
  • Tracking work plans and milestones
  • Coordinating with international partners and participating countries
  • Maintaining technical documentation and programme records

2. Data Management and Quality Assurance

The role requires maintaining high-quality education datasets by:

  • Managing learning assessment databases
  • Supporting data collection and ingestion
  • Validating submitted datasets
  • Managing version control
  • Harmonizing metadata
  • Monitoring country submissions
  • Maintaining structured repositories
  • Supporting quality assurance procedures that improve reporting accuracy

3. Research and Analytical Support

The Associate Project Officer will also contribute to technical research by:

  • Preparing datasets for analysis
  • Supporting indicator computation
  • Checking analytical outputs
  • Conducting background research on international learning assessments
  • Reviewing education measurement frameworks
  • Drafting technical papers
  • Preparing governance documents
  • Supporting expert committees

4. Project Management Support

Daily project coordination responsibilities include:

  • Monitoring project progress
  • Supporting internal reporting
  • Coordinating technical deliverables
  • Tracking implementation timelines
  • Identifying project bottlenecks
  • Assisting procurement activities
  • Supporting contract administration

5. Capacity Building and Knowledge Management

The selected candidate will help strengthen education systems by:

  • Preparing training materials
  • Developing technical guidance documents
  • Supporting workshops and consultations
  • Coordinating expert meetings
  • Maintaining knowledge repositories
  • Preparing international meeting materials
  • Facilitating coordination across UNESCO teams

6. Partnerships and Resource Mobilization

Responsibilities also include supporting organizational growth through:

  • Researching partnership opportunities
  • Identifying funding opportunities
  • Supporting grant and project proposal development
  • Preparing analytical background materials

Required Qualifications

Applicants should possess the following qualifications.

Education

Applicants must hold an advanced university degree (Master’s degree or equivalent) in one of the following fields:

  • Statistics
  • Economics
  • Data Science
  • Demography
  • Applied Mathematics
  • Related quantitative disciplines
  • Social sciences with significant applied statistics components

Professional Experience

Candidates should have:

  • At least two years of professional experience
  • Experience in data management
  • Experience in research
  • Experience supporting projects or programmes
  • Database management experience
  • Data processing experience
  • Experience supporting complex multi-component programmes

Required Skills

Successful applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong knowledge of education and assessment concepts
  • Understanding of statistical methods
  • Database management knowledge
  • Data-processing expertise
  • Research synthesis skills
  • Excellent documentation abilities
  • Strong organizational skills
  • Ability to coordinate multiple work streams
  • Excellent written communication
  • Excellent verbal communication
  • Technical writing skills

Language Requirement

Applicants must possess:

  • Excellent written English
  • Excellent spoken English

Desirable Qualifications

Preference may be given to applicants with:

  • Specialization in educational assessment
  • Learning measurement
  • International development
  • Experience working with education datasets
  • International assessment experience
  • Multi-country project experience
  • Workshop organization experience
  • Contract management experience
  • United Nations experience
  • Financial tracking experience
  • Project planning software knowledge

Additional language skills are considered an advantage:

  • French
  • Spanish
  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • Russian

UNESCO Core Values

Applicants are expected to demonstrate:

  • Commitment to the Organization
  • Integrity
  • Respect for Diversity
  • Professionalism
  • Accountability
  • Innovation
  • Teamwork
  • Planning and Organization
  • Results Focus
  • Knowledge Sharing

Salary and Benefits

UNESCO offers an internationally competitive compensation package.

The approximate annual starting salary is:

USD 75,325

Additional benefits may include:

  • 30 days annual leave
  • Medical insurance
  • Pension plan
  • Family allowance (where applicable)
  • International professional work environment
  • Career development opportunities
  • Exposure to global education policy initiatives

Selection Process

The recruitment process may include:

  1. Online application submission
  2. Written assessments
  3. Pre-recorded video interview
  4. Panel interview
  5. Reference checks
  6. Additional documentation verification

Applicants should ensure all information submitted is complete and accurate, as applications cannot be modified after submission.

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity allows professionals to contribute directly to improving education outcomes worldwide while working alongside leading experts in international education statistics.

Benefits of joining UNESCO include:

  • Contributing to Sustainable Development Goal 4
  • Working on internationally recognized education indicators
  • Collaborating with governments and global partners
  • Developing expertise in education data and policy
  • Building an international career within the United Nations system
  • Working in a multicultural and diverse environment

Diversity and Equal Opportunity

UNESCO strongly encourages applications from:

  • Women
  • Candidates from underrepresented Member States
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Indigenous peoples
  • Minority groups
  • Professionals from diverse cultural backgrounds

UNESCO follows a zero-tolerance policy regarding harassment and maintains equal employment opportunities for all qualified applicants.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 8 July 2026 (Midnight Paris Time)

Interested candidates should complete their applications before the deadline through the official UNESCO Careers portal. Ensure all required information and supporting documents are submitted before finalizing the application.

Apply Here

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

 

The post Associate Project Officer (Learning Assessments) at UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS), Montreal, Canada: Apply for an International Education Data and Research Career appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 in Japan | Up to €30,000 Funding for European Researchers
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026, offering outstanding European researchers the opportunity to conduct fully funded international research in Japan for periods ranging from three months to one year. The Canon Foundation in Europe annually awards up to 15 fellowships to highly qualified researchers seeking to strengthen international collaboration, […]

The post Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 in Japan | Up to €30,000 Funding for European Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026, offering outstanding European researchers the opportunity to conduct fully funded international research in Japan for periods ranging from three months to one year.

The Canon Foundation in Europe annually awards up to 15 fellowships to highly qualified researchers seeking to strengthen international collaboration, advance innovative research, and gain valuable global academic and professional experience.

One of the biggest strengths of this programme is its flexibility. Fellows are free to choose their own host institutions in Japan and may conduct research in any academic or professional field without thematic restrictions.

With funding support of up to €30,000 per year, the Canon Foundation Fellowship is considered one of the leading international research mobility programmes supporting European researchers in Japan.

About the Canon Foundation Research Fellowships

The Canon Foundation in Europe established this fellowship programme to encourage scientific, academic, and professional exchange between Europe and Japan. The programme supports international collaboration across all disciplines while promoting cross-cultural understanding and long-term research partnerships.

Each year, selected fellows receive support to pursue independent research projects at Japanese universities, laboratories, research centres, companies, government institutions, or professional organisations.

Unlike many fellowship programmes, applicants are not required to be currently employed or enrolled at a university at the time of application. This makes the opportunity highly accessible to independent researchers and professionals outside traditional academic settings.

The fellowship supports all research areas, including but not limited to:

  • Engineering and Technology
  • Artificial Intelligence and Data Science
  • Medical and Health Sciences
  • Climate and Environmental Studies
  • Economics and Business
  • Social Sciences
  • Humanities
  • Education
  • Public Policy
  • Architecture and Urban Development
  • Agriculture and Food Sciences
  • Physics and Chemistry
  • Arts and Cultural Studies

Fellowship Duration

The Canon Foundation Research Fellowship offers flexible research durations:

  • Minimum duration: 3 months
  • Maximum duration: 12 months

Research projects may begin anytime between January 2027 and December 2027. Fellowship activities may continue into 2028 depending on the approved project schedule.

Financial Benefits

The Canon Foundation provides generous funding support of up to €30,000 per year. Shorter fellowship periods receive pro-rated funding.

The fellowship grant can be used for:

  • Living expenses
  • International travel costs
  • Accommodation
  • Insurance
  • Research materials and supplies
  • Laboratory or fieldwork expenses
  • Books and academic resources
  • Other professional and research-related costs

Importantly, the Foundation does not impose strict spending restrictions, giving fellows flexibility in managing their research budgets.

Eligibility Criteria

The Canon Foundation Research Fellowship is open to European researchers wishing to conduct research in Japan.

Eligible regions include:

  • European Union countries
  • United Kingdom
  • Switzerland
  • Israel
  • Turkey
  • Balkan countries
  • Baltic countries

Applicants should hold at least:

  • A Master’s degree, or
  • A PhD degree

The qualification should typically have been obtained within the last ten years before applying.

However, applicants who completed their qualifications more than ten years ago may still be considered if they provide additional supporting information explaining their circumstances.

Key Features of the Fellowship

1. No Restrictions on Research Fields

Researchers from all academic and professional disciplines are eligible to apply.

2. Freedom to Choose Host Institution

Applicants may independently select their preferred host institution in Japan, including universities, laboratories, companies, government institutions, and research organisations.

The Foundation does not assign placements or provide host matching services, so applicants must arrange their host institution themselves.

3. Open Beyond Academia

The fellowship welcomes applications from professionals working in:

  • Academia
  • Industry
  • Government institutions
  • Commercial organisations
  • Professional organisations

4. No Current Employment Requirement

Applicants do not need to be enrolled at a university or employed by an organisation when applying.

5. Priority for First-Time Research Experience in Japan

Preference is generally given to applicants travelling to Japan for the first time rather than those extending an existing stay abroad.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must upload the following documents through the online application portal:

Mandatory Documents

  1. Research Plan (maximum 2 A4 pages)
    • Research objectives
    • Methodology
    • Expected outcomes
    • Estimated total costs
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Highest Academic Degree Certificate
  4. Passport-size Photograph
  5. Publications or Professional Achievements List
    • Chronological format
    • Include all authors
    • Publication year
    • Page references
  6. Two Reference Letters
    • From academic or professional referees familiar with the applicant’s qualifications and work
  7. Host Institution Confirmation Letter
    • Confirmation of support
    • Availability of facilities
    • Description of supervision arrangements

Important Information About Missing Documents

Applicants whose reference letters or host institution confirmation letters are not ready by the deadline should still submit their application before the official closing date.

Missing documents may be uploaded until:

30 September 2026, 23:59 CET

Alternatively, referees and host supervisors may directly email their letters to:

canon.foundation@canon-europe.com

Applicants must remember to re-submit their application after uploading additional documents.

Application Deadline

The application deadline for the Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 is:

15 September 2026, 23:59 CET

Selection Process and Success Rate

The Canon Foundation Fellowship is highly competitive, with an average success rate of approximately 10%.

Applications are evaluated based on:

  • Quality of the research proposal
  • Strength of recommendation letters
  • Feasibility of the research project
  • Support from the host institution

The Foundation states that funding decisions are not influenced by:

  • Gender
  • Age
  • Nationality

Successful applicants will be notified by mid-December 2026.

Important Conditions

Applicants should carefully note the following conditions:

  • The Foundation does not assist applicants in finding host institutions
  • Applicants holding concurrent research grants from other organisations are generally not eligible
  • Priority is given to applicants beginning new international research stays
  • Applications must be submitted through the official online portal

Why You Should Apply

The Canon Foundation Research Fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity to conduct fully funded international research while building meaningful collaborations between Europe and Japan.

Selected fellows benefit from:

  • Significant financial support
  • International research exposure
  • Flexible research arrangements
  • Freedom to choose host institutions
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Enhanced global career prospects
  • Cross-cultural collaboration and learning

Researchers seeking international academic and professional experience in Japan should strongly consider applying for this prestigious fellowship programme.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 in Japan.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post Canon Foundation Research Fellowships 2026 in Japan | Up to €30,000 Funding for European Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNFPA Young Creators Lab 2026: Fully Online Regional Programme for Young Digital Creators Across Asia and the Pacific
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the 2026 UNFPA Young Creators Lab, an exciting virtual regional programme designed to empower young creators and youth-led organizations across Asia and the Pacific with the skills needed to produce reliable, ethical, inclusive, and rights-based digital content. Organized by United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), the Young Creators Lab aims […]

The post UNFPA Young Creators Lab 2026: Fully Online Regional Programme for Young Digital Creators Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the 2026 UNFPA Young Creators Lab, an exciting virtual regional programme designed to empower young creators and youth-led organizations across Asia and the Pacific with the skills needed to produce reliable, ethical, inclusive, and rights-based digital content.

Organized by United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), the Young Creators Lab aims to equip the next generation of digital storytellers, advocates, influencers, educators, and youth leaders with practical tools to tackle misinformation and create impactful online content around comprehensive sexuality education, gender equality, sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR), online safety, healthy relationships, and youth empowerment.

As young people increasingly rely on social media platforms, short-form videos, and digital creators for information about health, relationships, gender, and rights, the need for accurate and responsible online content has become more important than ever. The UNFPA Young Creators Lab responds to this urgent need by building the capacity of young creators to lead positive digital change across the region.

The programme will run fully online from August to October 2026 and will feature nine live interactive sessions delivered every Tuesday at 11:00 AM Bangkok time.

The application deadline is 17 July 2026.

About the UNFPA Young Creators Lab

The UNFPA Young Creators Lab is a regional virtual learning and mentorship programme focused on helping young creators produce trustworthy and engaging digital content for young audiences.

The programme recognizes that digital platforms are now one of the most influential spaces where young people learn about:

  • Their bodies and health
  • Relationships and consent
  • Gender equality
  • Sexual and reproductive health
  • Rights and inclusion
  • Mental wellbeing
  • Identity and self-expression

However, alongside the opportunities created by digital media, online spaces also contain misinformation, harmful stereotypes, deepfakes, technology-facilitated gender-based violence, unsafe online practices, and misleading health content.

To address these challenges, UNFPA has created this innovative learning platform to help young creators become responsible digital educators and advocates.

Programme Duration and Format

The 2026 Young Creators Lab will run from August to October 2026 as a fully online programme.

Participants will attend:

  • Nine live virtual learning sessions
  • Interactive quizzes after every session
  • Sprint activities and collaborative learning exercises
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Community engagement activities
  • Optional capstone project development

All sessions will take place on Tuesdays at 11:00 AM Bangkok time.

What Participants Will Learn

The programme is structured around eight major learning pillars that focus on modern digital communication, ethical content creation, online safety, advocacy, and audience engagement.

1. Digital Comprehensive Sexuality Education Content Production
