Participants will learn how to create engaging, accurate, youth-friendly digital content focused on comprehensive sexuality education and life skills.

2. Messaging for Gender Equality and Positive Masculinity

The Lab will help creators understand how to produce content that promotes healthy masculinity, respectful relationships, inclusion, and gender equality.

3. AI Fluency for Creators

Young creators will gain practical exposure to artificial intelligence tools and learn how AI can support responsible content creation, digital storytelling, and communication.

4. Critical Assessment of Misinformation

Participants will learn how to identify misinformation, verify facts, evaluate online claims, and counter harmful narratives related to health and rights.

5. Technology-Facilitated Gender-Based Violence Prevention and Safety by Design

The programme will teach online safety principles and strategies for protecting creators and audiences from digital abuse, harassment, and harmful online practices.

6. Universal Design Principles for the Digital Era

Participants will explore inclusive and accessible content design practices to ensure digital materials can reach broader and more diverse audiences.

7. Audience Growth and the Algorithm

Creators will learn strategies for audience engagement, platform growth, visibility, and understanding how social media algorithms impact content performance.

8. Online Voice, Advocacy and Amplification

The programme will support participants in strengthening their digital advocacy skills, public voice, online campaigns, and community impact.

Capstone Project Opportunity

In addition to the training sessions, participants can choose to join an optional capstone project track.

Under mentor guidance, participants may develop:

  • A comprehensive sexuality education campaign
  • Digital educational content
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Multi-creator collaborations
  • Amplification toolkits
  • Social media storytelling initiatives

Selected capstone projects will be showcased during the programme’s Premiere Day event.

Benefits of the UNFPA Young Creators Lab

Participants selected for the programme will receive numerous professional and educational benefits, including:

Practical Digital Media Skills

Participants will gain hands-on skills in content production, advocacy, digital storytelling, AI literacy, and audience engagement.

Regional Networking Opportunities

Creators from different countries across Asia and the Pacific will connect, collaborate, and exchange ideas.

Mentorship and Guidance

Participants will receive mentorship support while developing projects and strengthening their online impact.

Exposure to International Learning

The programme provides direct learning opportunities connected to a United Nations agency working globally on youth empowerment and reproductive health rights.

UNFPA Completion Certificate

Participants who successfully complete the programme requirements will receive the UNFPA Young Creators Lab Completion Certificate.

Opportunity to Showcase Projects

Capstone projects may be featured during Premiere Day, providing visibility and recognition for participants’ work.

Who Should Apply?

The programme welcomes:

  • Young creators aged 18 to 29
  • Youth-led organizations
  • Emerging digital creators
  • Micro creators
  • Advocacy content creators
  • Youth educators
  • Social media storytellers
  • Young campaigners and communicators

Importantly, follower count is NOT a selection criterion.

Applicants do not need to be famous influencers or established creators to qualify.

Priority will be given to applicants interested in:

  • Comprehensive sexuality education
  • Sexual and reproductive health and rights
  • Gender equality
  • Healthy relationships
  • Ethical digital communication
  • Positive masculinity
  • Safe online engagement

The programme also aims to ensure strong representation across:

  • Geography
  • Gender
  • Disability inclusion
  • Diverse youth communities across Asia and the Pacific

Selection Criteria

UNFPA is looking for applicants who demonstrate:

  • Passion for positive digital impact
  • Ethical and responsible online communication
  • Interest in youth-focused advocacy
  • Commitment to inclusive and safe content creation
  • Willingness to learn and collaborate

Certification Requirements

To graduate from the programme and receive the UNFPA Young Creators Lab Completion Certificate, participants must:

  • Attend sessions
  • Pass all quizzes
  • Complete sprint assignments
  • Follow programme community guidelines

The capstone project is optional and not mandatory for certification.

How to Apply

Application Deadline: 17 July 2026

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the UNFPA Young Creators Lab 2026.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post UNFPA Young Creators Lab 2026: Fully Online Regional Programme for Young Digital Creators Across Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the SheConnects Digital Accelerator Africa Round 2: Funding and Partnership Opportunity to Scale Women’s Digital Empowerment in Sub-Saharan Africa
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Organizations working to bridge the digital gender gap across Africa are invited to apply for Round 2 of the SheConnects Digital Accelerator: Africa, an initiative designed to support innovative partnerships that empower women and girls through digital technologies. Managed by CARE in partnership with the GSMA Foundation and supported by the Gates Foundation, the SheConnects […]

The post Apply for the SheConnects Digital Accelerator Africa Round 2: Funding and Partnership Opportunity to Scale Women’s Digital Empowerment in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to bridge the digital gender gap across Africa are invited to apply for Round 2 of the SheConnects Digital Accelerator: Africa, an initiative designed to support innovative partnerships that empower women and girls through digital technologies.

Managed by CARE in partnership with the GSMA Foundation and supported by the Gates Foundation, the SheConnects Digital Accelerator aims to identify and scale proven, high-impact solutions that enable women and girls to fully participate in the digital economy.

Round 2 officially opens 20 May 2026 and focuses on Partnerships for Women’s Digital Empowerment at Scale, providing an opportunity for organizations to expand impactful digital initiatives while contributing to a more inclusive digital ecosystem across Sub-Saharan Africa.

Application Status

Applications are now open.

Applicants are encouraged to review the official Request for Applications and submit their proposals through the official application process.

About the SheConnects Digital Accelerator

The SheConnects Digital Accelerator: Africa was established to address one of the continent’s most pressing development challenges—the digital gender divide.

Across many parts of Sub-Saharan Africa, women and girls continue to face barriers to accessing digital technologies, affordable internet, digital skills, and online economic opportunities. These challenges limit their participation in education, entrepreneurship, employment, financial inclusion, and broader social development.

The Accelerator seeks to change this by supporting organizations with tested solutions that can be expanded to reach more women and girls.

Rather than focusing solely on technology, the programme emphasizes partnerships, evidence generation, and scalable innovation capable of creating lasting systemic change.

Round 2 Theme

The second round of the Accelerator is centered on:

Partnerships for Women’s Digital Empowerment at Scale

Successful applicants will be organizations or partnerships capable of delivering impactful digital solutions that improve women’s participation in the digital world while generating evidence that can influence future policies and digital inclusion strategies.

Programme Objectives

The SheConnects Digital Accelerator seeks to:

  • Increase meaningful digital adoption among women and girls.
  • Improve access to the internet and digital services.
  • Strengthen women’s economic opportunities through technology.
  • Support innovative partnerships across sectors.
  • Generate evidence on effective digital inclusion strategies.
  • Promote south-to-south knowledge sharing.
  • Strengthen inclusive digital ecosystems across Africa.
  • Influence future digital policies and programming.

What the Accelerator Supports

The programme is looking for proven solutions that can be expanded to create greater impact.

Potential initiatives may focus on:

  • Digital literacy and skills development.
  • Women’s access to affordable internet.
  • Digital entrepreneurship.
  • Financial inclusion through digital platforms.
  • Digital livelihoods.
  • Technology-enabled education.
  • Women’s online safety.
  • Digital innovation.
  • Community-based digital inclusion initiatives.
  • Partnerships that improve access to digital opportunities.

Projects should demonstrate clear evidence of impact and strong potential for scaling.

Why Women’s Digital Inclusion Matters

Digital technologies have become essential for participating in modern economies and societies.

When women have equitable access to digital tools and internet connectivity, they gain opportunities to:

  • Access education.
  • Start and grow businesses.
  • Improve household incomes.
  • Access healthcare information.
  • Participate in financial services.
  • Build professional networks.
  • Strengthen resilience during economic shocks.

Closing the digital gender gap also contributes to broader economic growth and sustainable development across Africa.

Partnership Approach

One of the distinguishing features of the Accelerator is its emphasis on collaboration.

Organizations are encouraged to build partnerships that bring together:

  • Civil society organizations.
  • Technology companies.
  • Government institutions.
  • Research organizations.
  • Innovation hubs.
  • Community-based organizations.
  • Private sector partners.

These collaborations help ensure solutions are sustainable, scalable, and capable of influencing digital ecosystems.

Round 1 Success

During the first round, CARE partnered with four African organizations that are advancing women’s meaningful use of digital technologies:

  • Centre for Information Technology and Development
  • KICTANet
  • Smart Regional Consultants
  • TechHer

These organizations are implementing innovative initiatives designed to improve digital access, strengthen livelihoods, and generate valuable evidence that can shape future digital inclusion efforts across the continent.

Programme Partners

The Accelerator is delivered through a partnership between:

  • CARE
  • GSMA Foundation

with financial support from:

  • Gates Foundation

Together, these organizations are working to accelerate digital inclusion and empower women across Sub-Saharan Africa through innovation, collaboration, and evidence-based programming.

Benefits of Participating

Organizations selected for the Accelerator can benefit from:

  • Support to scale proven digital solutions.
  • Opportunities to build strategic partnerships.
  • Increased visibility for innovative initiatives.
  • Access to learning and knowledge-sharing networks.
  • Contribution to research and evidence generation.
  • Opportunities to influence digital inclusion policies.
  • Strengthened organizational capacity.
  • Regional collaboration across Sub-Saharan Africa.

Why You Should Apply

If your organization is already delivering impactful digital solutions that empower women and girls, the SheConnects Digital Accelerator offers an opportunity to expand your reach, strengthen partnerships, and contribute to transforming digital inclusion across Africa.

Through collaboration, innovation, and evidence-based learning, participating organizations can help shape a future where women and girls have equal opportunities to thrive in the digital economy.

Interested organizations should review the official Request for Applications and submit their proposals through the official SheConnects Digital Accelerator application portal.

Apply Here 

For more information on this grant opportunity visit here.

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here

The post Apply for the SheConnects Digital Accelerator Africa Round 2: Funding and Partnership Opportunity to Scale Women’s Digital Empowerment in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
